content: Clinical management: preconception counseling for women of childbearing potential on teratogenic medications; contraception requirements (isotretinoin iPLEDGE program requires two forms); medication switching before conception (ACE inhibitors to labetalol/nifedipine; warfarin to LMWH; valproate to lamotrigine/levetiracetam if possible); folic acid supplementation (4mg/day for women on antiepileptics). If exposure occurs: determine gestational age, assess specific drug risk, discuss options (continuation with monitoring, termination). Risk communication: absolute vs relative risk important (baseline malformation rate 3-4%; valproate increases to 10%); most exposures don't cause defects. Resources: TERIS, MotherToBaby, Organization of Teratology Information Services provide evidence-based risk assessments.
